title: paullinia ferruginea
intro: Paullinia ferruginea, also known as the "ironwood" or "ferrugineous paullinia," is a plant species belonging to the Sapindaceae family. It is primarily found in tropical regions, including South America and Africa, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Traditionally, Paullinia ferruginea is prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
